criteria_results.description: The `users` PostgreSQL table has a `role` text column that is NOT NULL with default 'PM'. The `User` type in shared/schema.ts includes `role: string`.
criteria_results.evidence: shared/schema.ts lines 8 and 43 show the 'role' column definition with default 'PM' and its inclusion in the User type.

criteria_results.description: GET /api/auth/me returns `{ id, username, role }` for an authenticated user; POST /api/auth/login success response includes `role`.
criteria_results.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 41 and 61-62 show the 'role' field being returned in both login and /me responses.

criteria_results.description: PUT /api/projects/:id, POST /api/projects, DELETE /api/projects/:id, and POST /api/files each have `requireRole('PM','admin')` middleware in their handler chain after `requireAuth`.
criteria_results.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 71, 88, 102, 120, and 139 show the requireRole middleware correctly applied to all write endpoints.

criteria_results.description: Logging in as `admin` with password `2Park` succeeds — the user exists in the database with role 'admin'. seedAdminUser() creates this user on startup if not found.
criteria_results.evidence: server/auth.ts lines 96-114 implements seedAdminUser with the correct credentials and role. server/index.ts line 94 calls it before registering routes.

criteria_results.description: GET /api/admin/users and PUT /api/admin/users/:id/role both require role 'admin' — a PM or TECH user receives 403.
criteria_results.evidence: server/routes.ts lines 177 and 182 show both admin endpoints protected with requireRole('admin').

criteria_results.description: In ObjectRenderer.tsx, the Rnd component receives `disableDragging={isTech}` and `enableResizing={isTech ? {} : ...}` where isTech is derived from useCurrentUser().data?.role === 'TECH'.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/components/editor/Canvas/ObjectRenderer.tsx lines 152 and 153 show the correct props being passed to Rnd based on the isTech flag.

criteria_results.description: In PropertiesPanel.tsx, when isTech is true AND selectedObject.status === 'ISSUE', a section rendering `selectedObject.issueDescription` is present in the JSX.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/components/editor/PropertiesPanel.tsx lines 156-160 show the conditional rendering of issueDescription for TECH users when an ISSUE status is present.

criteria_results.description: File client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx exists and contains a user table with role selectors. The /admin route is registered in App.tsx.
criteria_results.evidence: client/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx exists and implements the user management UI. client/src/App.tsx line 71 registers the /admin route with role-based protection.

suggestions: The implementation also correctly restricted other UI elements for TECH users in Canvas.tsx, Toolbar.tsx, ObjectToolbar.tsx, and LayerPanel.tsx which goes beyond the minimum criteria and ensures a better UX.
